calendar.matters:
Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement And Mutual General Release, To Modify The Separate Share Trust For Ulric Riccardo Dunbar And The Separate Share Trust For Stella Ruby Dunbar To Conform To Settlement Agreement, And For Payment Of The Guardian Ad Litem
Off Calendar Apr-23-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bart Schenone for John Sahlin.
Monica Dell'Osso (Guardian Ad Litem) for Ulric Dunbar and Stella Dunbar (Minors).
Konstantine Demiris for Catherine Suarez-Dunbar.

The Court questions whether Mr. Demiris' Client has any continuing concerns. Mr. Demiris stated that his Client has no concerns, that they have reviewed the matter thoroughly, that he has been in communication with Counsel and apologized for not being present at the last hearing stating that it was his understanding that the matter would proceed based on the agreement of Counsel. The Court noted that it has some concerns about Ms. Suarez-Dunbar being the Trustee given the history of this, but that Ms. Dell'Osso is on board and all Counsel is here. The Court granted the Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement And Mutual General Release, To Modify The Separate Share Trust For Ulric Riccardo Dunbar And The Separate Share Trust For Stella Ruby Dunbar To Conform To Settlement Agreement, And For Payment Of The Guardian Ad Litem
Off Calendar Feb-19-2019 Continued To Apr-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bart Schenone for John Sahlin (Petitioner/Trustee).
Monica Dell'Osso Guardian Ad Litem for Stella Dunbar and Ulric Dunbar (Minors).

Mr. Schenone stated that he was expecting Mr. Demiris to be present and that he was not sure why he isn't here. The Court raised concern with respect to the suggestion that there was no time to speak with the Minors, stated that Stella is old enough to have some view, raised the issue of conflict with respect to the Minor's Mother pointing out that she has a lot to gain, questions the powers of the Trust Protector to amend or terminate although the trusts say they are irrevocable, stated that the powers in question where not mentioned in the settlement agreement, etc. Ms. Dell'Osso stated that she has not spoken directly with the Minors, that she spoke extensively with the Parents at mediation, and that she has a sense that the Parents have been very candid with the Minors about the settlement and the litigation. The Court stated that it would not be so concerned if the Mother wasn't the adversary. Ms. Dell' Osso also stated that Catherine (Mother of the Minors) is receiving $250,000.00 from each of the Minor's trusts, that each of the Minor's trusts will receive 1.5 million dollars, that she did not want to burden the Minors with the emotional and psychological toll of litigation between the family members, that the settlement avoids significant fees and cost of litigation, that this is in the best interest of the Children, and that it is a fair and very reasonable settlement. The Court stated that it is not comfortable with the language as it stands. Mr. Schenone stated that he has no problem with revising the language, that special needs trust ("SNT") language was in the original trust document, that they do not feel a SNT is appropriate for Stella, but it is appropriate for Ulric in case something develops in the future and that they want the Trust Protector to have the ability to meet changing circumstances. Ms. Dell'Osso stated that the trusts can not be terminated without the signature of the Trustee and the Trust Protector, and spoke of how the Trust Protector is going to be paid. The Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement and Mutual General Release, etc. is continued to April 16, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to make changes to the language and for Ms. Dell'Osso to determine the appropriateness of talking with the Minors.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Cilley for John Sahlin (Trustee).

Mr. Cilley stated that the Trustee filed a petition for appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem, that initially Stacie Nelson was appointed and that opposing Counsel had objection to Stacie Nelson out of concern of the amount of fees her office would be charging. Mr. Cilley also stated that he has been working with opposing Counsel to select someone else, that they have settled on Monica Del'Osso, that he anticipates getting a petition filed in a couple of days and that they have discussed setting up mediation with Betty Epstein. The Petition for Instructions and Petition to Invalidate Amendment, etc. are continued to March 21, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Konstantine Demiris for Catherine Suarez-Dunbar (Petitioner).
James Ciley for John Sahlin (Trustee).

Mr. Ciley stated that Ms. Nelson is not present, but he is in contact with her, that she has presented her consent to the petition, that he told her she did not need to appear today and that he has not seen an order appointing her. The Court informed Mr. Ciley that the orders appointing Ms. Nelson as Guardian Ad Litem were signed and filed on August 25, 2017. Mr. Demiris stated that they are in agreement with Ms. Nelson's appointment. The Petition to Invalidate Amendment and Restatement to Trust and the Petition for Instructions are continued to November 6, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Nelson is required to appear on that date.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
